title: FEE_CLAIM_RESULT
description: Response from the Keeper to a FEE_CLAIM. Covers both acceptance and rejection in a single message type via the status field. On ACCEPTED, this message carries the same semantics as FEE_RELEASE: the Referee may proceed to collect payment. On REJECTED, the rejection_reason field indicates why the claim was denied. See RFC-0001 Section 6.23 for details.

incident_id.description: Incident identifier. Echoed from the corresponding FEE_CLAIM.

referee_id.description: Terminal ID of the Referee that sent the FEE_CLAIM.

status.description: ACCEPTED: the claim is approved and the escrowed amount is released. REJECTED: the claim is denied; see rejection_reason.

released_amount.description: (Present when status is ACCEPTED) Amount released from escrow to the Referee. Bounded to at most 3 decimal places (every ISO 4217 currency, including BHD/KWD/OMR) so JCS number serialization round-trips exactly across implementations.

currency.description: Three-letter uppercase currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'JPY', 'EUR'). ISO 4217 is recommended but not enforced.

rejection_reason.description: (Present when status is REJECTED) Reason for rejection. TIMER_NOT_ELAPSED: the FEE_RELEASE timer has not yet expired. APPEAL_PENDING: a valid ASSESSMENT_APPEAL is currently pending. CERT_NOT_FOUND: cert_id was not recognized — not found in an ASSESSMENT_ISSUED anchor (Referee's own Keeper) or an INCIDENT_NOTICE(ASSESSMENT_COMPLETE) record (party Keeper in split-Keeper topology). ALREADY_RELEASED: payment for this incident has already been released. ESCROW_EXPIRED: the escrow was refunded to the depositing parties after the assessment deadline expired (STD-028); a late result confers no payment right through the escrow.

timer_expires_at.description: (Present when rejection_reason is TIMER_NOT_ELAPSED) Scheduled FEE_RELEASE timer expiry. Provided so the Referee can determine when to retry.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when this result was issued by the Keeper.
